description: 'noruwa' has two linked uses: a cohort passing through life at roughly the same time, and one interval in a line of descent. Context distinguishes 'mia noruwa' (my generation) from a count such as 'wi noruwa' (two generations). Exact years remain a separate measurement.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the upper teeth for dental 'n' and release into middle-height back rounded 'o'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then tighten the lip rounding for stressed high-back 'u'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then open into central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The tongue tap in the stressed middle syllable turns quickly into the final glide. That forward movement gives the three syllables a useful felt contour for people or intervals following one another.
usage_notes: A named law or study may set exact cohort boundaries; keep those criteria with the source. In ordinary Phi, the context shows whether 'noruwa' means contemporaries or a step between descent levels.

pillars.solarpunk-values: 'noruwa' gives long-term choices an audible span. A plan can ask what reaches later generations instead of speaking of the future as if no people live there.

semantic_domains.community: people grouped by a shared position in social time
semantic_domains.temporal: a cohort or interval within succession through time
